title: Close the current screen-core backing detachment regression on the hosted overlay path
summary: Stabilize the bare screen-core presentation after the orientation fixes by switching the visible rear backing to the thinner authored disconnect_surface and keeping the thicker screen_body hidden in the phase-1 direct-CUA lane.
reasoning: After 0.2.6, front-facing video orientation was correct again, which isolated the remaining visible defect back to geometry: the media plane and the black rear backing were separating. Live prefab inspection showed that the authored screen-core resource already carries a disconnect_surface almost flush behind screen_surface, while screen_body is a deeper cube farther back. The current hosted presentation code was following screen_body, which matches the detached black slab the operator still sees. Version 0.2.7 keeps the fix narrow by preferring disconnect_surface as the screen-core backdrop follower and hiding screen_body in this phase-1 presentation seam.

changes.summary: Changed the hosted screen-core backdrop presentation to prefer disconnect_surface instead of screen_body when the runtime overlay path is active.
changes.reasoning: disconnect_surface already matches the visible screen dimensions and sits close to the authored front screen, so it is the right rear backing for the current direct-CUA overlay contract.

changes.summary: Explicitly hid screen_body in the standalone CUA placeholder and runtime backdrop seam when it is not the chosen backing object.
changes.reasoning: Leaving the thicker body visible behind the overlay creates the detached-slab look even when playback and orientation are otherwise correct.

verification: Stamp version 0.2.7 into player.version.json and BuildRuntimeInfo.cs.
Build and deploy fa_cua_player.0.2.7.dll and fa_player_asset.0.2.7.assetbundle.
Validate the 0.2.7 release packet, including versioned live outputs and generated changelog artifacts.
knownIssues: Loop behavior still needs a dedicated follow-up if the operator continues to see single-style continuation under some loop settings.
Joystick and gaze focus remain intentionally deferred until the screen lane is fully stable.
